stories.title: Fable 5 on Track to Return This Week After 15-Day Government Ban
stories.summary: Axios reports Anthropic's Fable 5 model is on track to return to general access within days after negotiations with the US government progressed significantly. Mythos 5 was partially restored on June 27 for ~100 US companies and agencies, while Pentagon/NSA sign-off for broader restoration remains outstanding.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Scores 91.9% on Terminal-Bench, Beating Mythos 5 at 88%
stories.summary: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 as three tiers (Sol, Terra, Luna) in a government-approved limited preview. Sol's ultra multi-agent mode scored 91.9% on Terminal-Bench 2.1, the highest ever recorded. Even the cheapest tier Luna scores 82.5%, above Claude Opus 4.8.

stories.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Jalapeño: OpenAI's First Custom AI Chip
stories.summary: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, its first custom AI inference chip built with Broadcom in just nine months — the fastest ASIC development cycle ever. Early testing shows substantially better performance per watt than Nvidia alternatives, targeting initial deployment by end of 2026.

stories.title: Anthropic Accuses Alibaba of 28.8 Million Claude Distillation Attacks
stories.summary: Anthropic accused Alibaba's Qwen AI lab of running the largest known distillation attack, using 25,000 fake accounts to harvest 28.8 million Claude interactions over six weeks. The campaign targeted agentic reasoning and software engineering capabilities.

stories.title: Colorado AI Act Takes Effect June 30 — First Comprehensive State AI Law in the US
stories.summary: Colorado's Consumer Protections for Artificial Intelligence Act takes effect today, becoming the first comprehensive state AI law in the US. A May 2026 amendment added a carve-out for algorithmic discrimination liability, shifting the regulatory landscape for AI deployers and developers.

stories.title: SpaceX Acquires Cursor for $60 Billion in Largest VC-Backed Startup Deal Ever
stories.summary: SpaceX announced a $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor), the largest VC-backed startup deal in history. Cursor generates ~$4B annualized revenue with 50,000+ enterprise clients. SpaceX and Cursor have been jointly training a coding model on the Colossus supercluster.

stories.title: Google Launches ARDS: Open Standard for AI Agents to Discover and Interact with Web Resources
stories.summary: Google launched the Agentic Resource Discovery Specification (ARDS), backed by Microsoft, GitHub, Hugging Face, NVIDIA, Amazon, Cisco, Salesforce, and Snowflake. The Apache 2.0-licensed standard enables AI agents to autonomously discover and interact with resources across the web.

stories.title: Ornith-1.0: Self-Improving Open-Source Models for Agentic Coding
stories.summary: DeepReinforce AI released Ornith-1.0, a family of self-improving open-source LLMs designed for agentic coding tasks. The models use self-scaffolding to autonomously build and refine their own tooling, representing a step toward AI systems that improve themselves.

stories.title: Qualcomm Reportedly in Talks to Acquire Tenstorrent for $8-10B to Bolster AI Chips
stories.summary: Qualcomm is in early talks to acquire AI chip startup Tenstorrent for $8-10 billion. Founded by chip veteran Jim Keller, Tenstorrent designs AI chips using the open RISC-V standard. The deal would give Qualcomm a major foothold in the AI hardware race against Nvidia and AMD.

stories.title: Micro-Agent: Beat Frontier Models with Collaboration Inside Model API
stories.summary: A new Micro-Agent approach demonstrates that smaller models collaborating within a model API can match or beat frontier model performance. The technique uses vLLM to orchestrate multiple smaller agents working together, offering a cost-effective alternative to expensive frontier models.
